name: Small uncontrolled ED pilot reported PHQ-9 improvement after daily tadalafil
statement: A 10-participant open-label pilot in men with ED and depressive symptoms reported statistically significant PHQ-9 improvement after 8 weeks of tadalafil 5 mg daily, but the single-arm design cannot separate tadalafil, expectancy, regression to the mean, or ED improvement effects.
population_or_context: Men with ED and PHQ-9 score at least 5.
quantitative_note: Mean PHQ-9 change was 3.60+/-3.27 and PHQ-15 change was 2.00+/-2.98; PHQ-9 improvement was reported as statistically significant (P<0.05).

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports n=10, tadalafil 5 mg daily for 8 weeks, PHQ-9/PHQ-15 changes, and non-significant BDNF change.

caveats: Participants had ED and depressive symptoms; not a healthy population.
population_profile: Ten men with ED and depressive symptoms; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 8 weeks.
indication_context: ED treatment with exploratory depression endpoint.
effect_estimate: PHQ-9 mean change 3.60+/-3.27 after treatment; reported P<0.05.
safety_findings: Detailed adverse-event extraction was not available from the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Very low directness because the sample had ED and depressive symptoms and was uncontrolled.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Single-arm open-label design, n=10, no control group, and ED improvement may mediate mood score changes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil exposure; no class transfer, but causal and population transfer are weak.
